estimated.note: Higher estimate includes unreported deaths in restricted areas
Slight variance due to delayed military casualty reports

parties.name: Iranian Civilians

estimated.note: Wide range: HRANA says 7,015; Iranian doctors network says 16,500-18,000; Iran International estimates up to 36,500. Trump cited 32,000 in State of the Union.

parties.arrestedNote: Mass arrests during Dec 2025–Jan 2026 protests. HRANA documented 41,800+ detained across 31 provinces.

parties.name: Iran

confirmed.source: Iran Health Ministry official figure (Day 25). Military casualty figures concealed by Iranian government. Health Ministry reported 1,500+ killed including 210+ children, 234 women. 300+ health/emergency facilities damaged. Last detailed update March 8 (~1,200 killed); cumulative updated to 1,500 by March 23. Day 25 figures unavailable due to internet blackout.
confirmed.confirmedMilitaryNote: Iran does not publish military casualty figures. The 0 reflects concealment, not absence of military deaths.

estimated.source: HRANA (Human Rights Activists News Agency) — Day 23 report (March 22). 1,407 civilians killed (including 214 children, 234 women), 1,138 military, 657 unclassified. Total 3,202. Over 15% of fatalities are children.
estimated.note: HRANA documents via field reports, local contacts, medical sources, and civil society networks. By Day 4 alone, HRANA counted 1,097 civilians killed (including 181 children). Iran International reported HRANA confirmed 1,407+ civilian deaths including 214 children as of Day 23. Military figure (1,138) + unclassified (657) = 1,795 non-civilian. 65 schools and 77+ healthcare facilities damaged. Internet at ~4% — independent verification near-impossible. ~40 officials killed including Armed Forces CoS Bagheri, IRGC commanders Salami, Rashid, Hajizadeh.

rumored.note: Hengaw 6th report (March 20): 5,900 killed — 5,305 military, 595 civilians (127 minors, 168 women) across 184 cities in 26 provinces. IDF Brig Gen Defrin claims 6,000+ IRGC killed and 15,000+ wounded. With ongoing strikes through Day 24, upper range approaching 9,000. Internet blackout prevents independent verification.

socialSources.claim: 6th report (March 20): 5,900 killed — 5,305 military, 595 civilians (127 minors, 168 women). 184 cities across 26 provinces targeted. Iranian security institutions systematically conceal casualty figures.
5th report (March 17): 5,300 killed — 4,789 military, 511 civilians. 120 minors, 160 women among dead. 178 cities across 25 provinces affected.
Day 23: 1,407 civilians killed including 214 children. More than 15% of total fatalities are children. Military casualties significantly higher than civilian count.
Over 1,400 civilians killed in first three weeks per HRANA documentation. True toll likely far higher due to internet blackout and government concealment.
IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed, 15,000 wounded, 70% of Iran's missile launchers destroyed. Plans through Passover (3+ more weeks).
Experts say actual death toll 'could be far greater as more details are released' — Iran's internet blackout prevents verification.
Iran's true casualty figures unknown as internet blackout hampers monitors. Connection worse than ever before — extremely difficult to get accurate data.

parties.injuredNote: 18,551+ injured per Al Jazeera live tracker citing Iranian Red Crescent / Health Ministry (Day 25). 210+ children killed, 1,500+ under 18 injured. 300+ health/emergency facilities damaged. 82,000+ civilian structures damaged or destroyed per Iranian Red Crescent Society (March 24). Iran internet at ~4% — independent verification near-impossible.
parties.missingNote: 61 sailors still missing from IRIS Dena — 87 bodies recovered and 32 rescued from ~180 crew after US submarine USS Charlotte torpedoed the frigate off Sri Lanka coast (March 4).
parties.displacedNote: 3.2M displaced across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz and border regions per UNHCR. Shelter capacity exceeded.
parties.arrestedNote: 20 arrested in NW Iran for alleged cooperation with Israel. 18 arrested for ties to Iran International (March 16-17).

parties.name: Israel

confirmed.source: Alma Center daily reports / Israel Health Ministry / MDA — Day 25. 2 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon + 17 civilians in Israel. Key: 9 killed Beit Shemesh synagogue (March 1), 2 killed Ramat Gan (March 18, couple in 70s), 1 Filipino national Mary Ann De Vera (March 1), 1 Thai worker Moshav Adanim (March 18), 2 killed Yehud cluster bomb. 1 woman killed at Mahanayim Junction (March 24, Hezbollah rocket). 3 additional Palestinians killed in West Bank (Beit Awwa) by cluster debris — not in Israel count.

estimated.source: INSS / Statista compilation + Alma Center daily reports + Israeli emergency services
estimated.note: INSS at Tel Aviv University tracks 16 fatalities (14 civilian + 2 military) through March 18. Additional incidents after March 18: 1 Misgav Am (March 22), 1 Thai worker Moshav Adanim, 1 Mahanayim Junction (March 24, Hezbollah). Estimated 22 total including possible unreported deaths. 92% interception rate prevented far worse toll. Key incidents: 9 killed Beit Shemesh (Day 2), 2 killed Yehud cluster bomb (Day 10), 2 killed Ramat Gan (Day 18), 1 killed Mahanayim (Day 25).

rumored.note: Iranian state media claims significantly higher Israeli casualties — IRGC claims 40+ killed across 9 missile salvos. OSINT analysts report direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media. Israel's military censor restricts casualty reporting under wartime regulations.

socialSources.claim: Multiple videos show direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media — possible casualties suppressed by military censorship.
IRGC claims 40+ Israelis killed across 9 missile salvos — IDF only confirming 17.
2 killed in Ramat Gan as Iranian cluster missile hits multiple impact sites. Shrapnel rained on several neighborhoods.
Iranian missile strikes injure 180+ in Israel (Arad/Dimona), puncturing air defenses and shocking the public.

parties.injuredNote: 4,588+ hospitalized per Israel Health Ministry via Alma Center (March 24 evening). Includes 180+ in Dimona/Arad strikes (March 21-22), 12 injured in Bnei Brak cluster munition strike (Day 25, including 6 children), 4 lightly injured from northern Tel Aviv ballistic missile interception failure (Day 25), 4 injured including 2 infants from Negev Bedouin community missile fragment (Day 25), 48 IDF casualties in Lebanon operations (2 killed, 46 injured). MDA treated 1,576+ since war start. ~5,000 Israelis displaced.
parties.displacedNote: ~5,000 Israelis displaced from homes due to missile strikes per Israeli media reports.

parties.name: United States

confirmed.source: Pentagon / CENTCOM official count. 7 killed by enemy fire (6 Kuwait drone strike + Sgt. Pennington from Saudi wounds) + 6 KC-135 crew killed in Iraq crash (March 12). Does not include 2 non-combat deaths.

estimated.source: Pentagon / CENTCOM — 13 official + 2 non-combat deaths during operation
estimated.note: 15 total US deaths during Operation Epic Fury: (1) Kuwait drone strike, March 1 — 6 killed: Capt. Cody Khork (35), SFC Noah Tietjens (42), SFC Nicole Amor (39), Spc. Declan Coady (20), Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien (45), CW3 Robert Marzan (54), all 103rd Sustainment Command. (2) Sgt. Benjamin Pennington (26) — wounded March 1 at Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia, died March 8. (3) LCpl Kevin Melendez (~20, USMC) — died in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, non-hostile, under investigation. (4) Maj. Sorffly Davius (46, NY National Guard/NYPD officer) — died March 6 at Camp Buehring, Kuwait, health-related incident. (5) KC-135 crash, March 12 — 6 killed: Maj. John Klinner (33), Capt. Ariana Savino (31), Capt. Seth Koval (38), Capt. Curtis Angst (30), TSgt. Ashley Pruitt (34), TSgt. Tyler Simmons (28). Crash not due to hostile fire per CENTCOM. ~200 wounded across 7 countries (March 17), 10 seriously. 180+ returned to duty.

rumored.note: IRGC claims 650+ US killed/wounded in first 2 days (largely debunked by Jerusalem Post). Ali Larijani claimed US soldiers were captured — Pentagon explicitly denied. Iran state TV broadcast alleged captured US troops. Multiple IRGC claims later deleted after being disproven.

socialSources.claim: IRGC spokesperson claimed 650 US troops killed or wounded in first 2 days, including 160 at US military HQ in Bahrain. Claims largely disproven.
Claims Iran captured US soldiers. Pentagon explicitly denied any captures.
Sources in Bahrain report 12+ additional US wounded at 5th Fleet HQ not in official Pentagon count.

parties.injuredNote: ~200 wounded across 7 countries per CENTCOM (March 17). 180+ returned to duty, 10 seriously injured (2 reclassified). Wounds include burns, TBIs, shrapnel. Most injuries from one-way attack drones. Countries: Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Jordan, Bahrain, Iraq, Israel.

parties.name: Lebanon

confirmed.source: Lebanese Ministry of Public Health — March 24, Day 25. 1,072 killed including 121 children, 82 women, 42 healthcare workers. 2,966 wounded. Health Ministry does not separately break out Hezbollah fighter deaths.

estimated.source: Lebanese Health Ministry (1,072 confirmed, March 24) + Alma Center Hezbollah fighter estimates (~350 killed)
estimated.note: 1,072 confirmed by Health Ministry (March 24). Alma Center estimates 350+ Hezbollah fighters killed including ~1/3 Radwan special forces and senior commanders (Abu Khalil Barji, Hussein Makled). Hezbollah does not announce its dead — fighter deaths folded into Health Ministry civilian count. IDF destroyed all Litani River crossings, trapping ~150,000 civilians. Israel announced permanent 'security zone' to Litani. 1.5M displaced. Ground invasion ongoing south of Litani. Amnesty International: Israel deploying same playbook as 2024.

rumored.note: Hezbollah fighter casualties not separately broken out in Health Ministry figures — actual Hezbollah dead could be 300-400+. IDF ground invasion (launched March 16) expected to significantly increase casualties. Rescue teams unable to access multiple strike sites in southern Lebanon. Some villages entirely cut off.

socialSources.claim: 26 killed in last 24 hours across Beirut, Nabatieh, Baalbek, Bint Jbeil. 1 million displaced — 20% of Lebanon's population.
One million displaced nationwide. 130,000+ in 600+ collective shelters. Humanitarian access increasingly restricted.
IDF claims approximately 200 Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2. Hezbollah deliberately does not announce its dead.

parties.injuredNote: 2,966 wounded per Lebanese Health Ministry (March 24). 121 children, 82 women, 42 healthcare workers among dead. Over 1.5 million displaced. IDF ground operation ongoing south of Litani River. Israel announced permanent 'security zone' to Litani River.
parties.displacedNote: 1.5M displaced per Lebanese authorities as of March 24 — approximately 1 in 4 Lebanese. UN warned nearly a quarter of the population is uprooted.

parties.name: Gulf States (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, etc.)

confirmed.source: Al Jazeera tracker + regional government reports (March 24). UAE 8 killed (foreign nationals from Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, Palestine), Kuwait 6 (including 1 girl hit by shrapnel, 2 fire force officers), Bahrain 3 (1 Asian worker, 1 Bahraini woman, 1 woman killed March 10 in Manama), Saudi Arabia 2 (al-Kharj residential area), Oman 3. 1 additional Indian national minor injury in UAE Day 25 not yet counted in death toll.

estimated.source: Compiled from regional government reports and HRW (March 17)
estimated.note: Kuwait: 6 Kuwaiti killed (US soldiers counted under US), 32 injured. UAE: 8 killed (foreign nationals from Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh), 160 injured, 262 missiles detected (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted. Saudi Arabia: 2 killed (al-Kharj), 20 injured. Bahrain: 2 killed (1 Asian worker, 1 Bahraini woman), dozens injured. Oman: 3 killed, 15 injured. Qatar: 0 killed, 16 injured. Iran targeted Fairmont The Palm Hotel, Dubai International Financial Centre, airports.

rumored.note: HRW reports majority of Gulf civilian victims are migrant workers — deaths may be underreported due to foreign national status. Some Gulf nations restrict casualty reporting. Air defense interception rates (92-95%) prevented much worse toll. Iran launched 1,800+ missiles and drones at UAE alone.

socialSources.claim: At least 11 civilian deaths and 268 injuries across GCC states. 10 of those killed are foreign nationals. Migrant worker deaths likely underreported.
Fujairah oil hub struck 2 consecutive days. Saudi al-Kharj compound hit with casualties. Full picture unclear as some nations restrict reporting.

parties.injuredNote: 267+ injured across GCC states per Al Jazeera tracker. UAE: 160 wounded. Kuwait: 32 (dozens). Saudi Arabia: 20. Qatar: 16. Oman: 15 (5 per HRW + additional). Bahrain: dozens. HRW documented 268+ civilian injuries across GCC states. Majority of victims are migrant workers.

confirmed.source: Iraqi health authorities (March 24). At least 76 killed, mostly members of Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF) killed in US strikes on militia targets. Includes 15 killed in deadliest single strike of the conflict at Habbaniyah base, Anbar (March 24) — PMF Anbar commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji and 14 fighters confirmed killed.

estimated.source: Iraqi health authorities + The National + Kurdistan 24 reporting
estimated.note: Most casualties are PMF (Popular Mobilisation Forces) members killed in US strikes on militia targets. Day 25 Habbaniyah strike added 15 killed (including commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji) and 30 wounded. CWO Arnaud Frion (France) killed at Makhmour base counted under France.

confirmed.source: French Ministry of Armed Forces (March 13). CWO Arnaud Frion, age 42, 7th Battalion Chasseurs Alpins, 21-year veteran, killed by Iranian Shahed drone at Makhmour base near Erbil, Iraq. First European military death in the conflict. Married, father of one.

estimated.source: French government confirmed single fatality. Pro-Iranian group Ashab Al Kahf claimed responsibility.
estimated.note: CWO Arnaud Frion killed at French/Peshmerga base near Makhmour, Iraq (March 12-13). 7 French soldiers total wounded. No Peshmerga injured. Macron called attack 'unacceptable.' Iraq PM Sudani vowed to prevent recurrence. France has ~800 troops across Iraq/Syria in Operation Chammal.
